info.title: User Account Service API
info.description:
Manages user accounts, invitations, server profiles, and authentication
for the LegalDispatch platform.

## Authentication
Most endpoints require a JWT token passed via the API Gateway. The gateway
validates the token and forwards claims in the `X-User-Claims` header.
Internal service-to-service calls use the `X-Internal-Token` header.

## Error Responses
All errors follow a consistent JSON envelope:
```json
{ "code": "ERROR_CODE", "message": "Human-readable message", "request_id": "..." }
```

## Naming Convention
All JSON fields use **snake_case** naming.

get.description: Returns 200 OK if the process is running. Does **not** verify downstream dependencies — use `/ready` for that.
Returns 200 OK when the primary database is reachable. Returns 503 Service Unavailable otherwise. Also reports connectivity to the read replica, Redis cache, and RabbitMQ event bus.

post.description: Completes an invitation-based registration by creating the user account, credentials in the Auth Service, and an email verification token. The invitation token is consumed and cannot be reused.
Self-registration for independent process servers. No invitation required. Creates a user account with `role = process_server`, an empty server profile, and sends an email verification link.
Verifies a user's email using the one-time token sent to their inbox. Transitions the user status from `pending_verification` to `active`.

Invites a new operations staff member to the caller's partner organization. Validates the role, checks seat limits, and ensures no duplicate pending invitation exists. For `client_user` role, a valid `client_id` must be provided.

get.description: Returns all invitations for the caller's partner, optionally filtered by `status` (`pending`, `accepted`, `expired`, `revoked`). Includes the inviter's display name for each invitation.

post.description: Invites an independent process server to join the caller's partner network. Checks for existing active relationships and duplicate pending invitations.

delete.description: Revokes a pending invitation, preventing the recipient from registering. Only pending invitations belonging to the caller's partner can be revoked.

post.description: Resends the invitation email for a pending invitation. Expired or already-accepted invitations cannot be resent.

get.description: Returns a paginated list of all users belonging to the caller's partner organization. Supports optional filtering by `role`, `status`, and free-text `search` (matches name or email). Default page size is 20; maximum is 100.
Returns the full profile of a specific user within the caller's partner organization. Returns 404 if the user does not exist or belongs to a different partner.

put.description: Changes the role of a user within the caller's partner organization. **Required Role:** `partner_admin` only. Guards: cannot change your own role; cannot remove the last `partner_admin`.

post.description: Deactivates a user account (soft delete). The user loses access immediately and all active sessions are invalidated. Data and order history are retained for audit. **Required Role:** `partner_admin` only.
Reactivates a previously deactivated or suspended user account. Checks that reactivation would not exceed the partner's seat limit. **Required Role:** `partner_admin` only.

get.description: Returns the full profile of the currently authenticated user, including partner name, notification preferences, and account status. Any authenticated user can call this endpoint.

put.description: Updates the authenticated user's profile. Only provided (non-null) fields are changed. Updatable fields: `first_name`, `last_name`, `phone`, and `notification_preferences`. Email and role cannot be changed through this endpoint.

post.description: Upload a new avatar image for the current user. **Content-Type:** `multipart/form-data` with a `file` field. Accepted formats: JPEG, PNG, WebP. Maximum size: 5 MB.

Changes the authenticated user's password. Requires current password confirmation. The password change is delegated to the Auth Service, which verifies the current password before applying the update.

get.description: Returns the authenticated process server's public marketplace profile, including certifications, service areas, work preferences, and aggregate stats. Only accessible to users with the `process_server` role.

put.description: Updates the authenticated process server's marketplace profile. Only provided (non-null) fields are applied. Updatable fields: `display_name`, `bio`, `base_rate`, `availability_status`, and `work_preferences`.

Replaces all service coverage areas for the authenticated process server. Existing areas are deactivated and new areas are inserted atomically in a single transaction. Supported area types: `state`, `county`, `city`, `zip`, `radius`.

get.description: Returns all partner relationships for the authenticated process server, including pending invitations, active partnerships, and terminated relationships. Partner names are resolved from the Partner Management Service.

post.description: Accepts a pending partner relationship invitation. Sets the agreed rate and activates the relationship, enabling the server to receive assignments from this partner. The relationship must be in `pending` status.
Declines a pending partner relationship invitation. Sets the relationship status to `declined`. The relationship must be in `pending` status.

get.description: Returns a lightweight user identity record. Called by SOP Service, Filing Service, Operations Service, and Webhook Service.
Lightweight status check for the API Gateway to verify a user is active before forwarding requests.
Resolves a user identity during login when the partner context is known (e.g., custom domain login). **partner_id** is optional — omit for process servers and super admins.
Resolves all accounts tied to an email across all partners. Returns `single_account` or `multiple_accounts` resolution for the login partner selector.

get.summary: Get auth context for JWT construction
get.description: Returns the full role/partner context needed to build JWTs. Operations users receive a permission set; process servers receive a partner relationship list.

post.description: Validates a specific permission for a user in a partner context. Called by the API Gateway, SOP Service, and Filing Service.

get.summary: Get server profile with location data
get.description: Returns a process server's profile including **home_base_lat/lng** for distance calculations. These coordinates are internal-only and never exposed in public responses.
Returns servers with active relationships to the specified partner, sorted by coverage specificity, rating, then distance. Used by the Operations Service for the assignment view.

post.description: Creates the first admin user and invitation during partner onboarding. Called by the Partner Management Service.

get.description: Returns active user counts and pending invitation counts for seat limit enforcement. Called by the Partner Management Service.

post.description: Updates the user's last login timestamp and IP address. Called by the API Authentication Service after successful login.

get.description: Returns the user's preferred notification channels (email, SMS, push). Called by the Notification Service.
Returns email, phone, and full name for outbound notifications. Called by the Notification Service.

tags.name: Health
tags.description: Liveness and readiness probes for orchestrators (Kubernetes, ECS).

tags.name: Registration
tags.description: User account creation and email verification.

tags.name: Invitations
tags.description: Invite operations staff or process servers to join a partner.

tags.name: Users
tags.description: User profile management, role changes, and deactivation.

tags.name: Server Profiles
tags.description: Public marketplace profiles for process servers.
